items.name: /design-5
items.bodyPreview:
# Design — 5 variants (on-demand)

Scaffold design review **only because this command was invoked**.

## Steps

1. Read `.cursor/skills/workspace-design-preview-gate/SKILL.md`.
2. Read `.cursor/rules/dev-workspace.mdc` §3.
3. Add mockups under **System → Design Template** (P0004/P0020) or `/design-preview/<feature>` (P0008-style) if the user requested a route.
4. Produce **≥5 distinct variants**; wait for `Design: V1`…`V5` before production lock-in.
5. After lock: delete mocks, registry entries, preview CSS; tab shows **No active designs**.

Do **not** use this command for bugfixes with no UX change.

items.name: /git
items.bodyPreview:
# Git (commit) — P00xx / E00xx

Keyword: **`Git P0004`** (replace code and product).

## Run

1. Read `.cursor/skills/p00xx-ship-keywords/SKILL.md` — keyword **Git**.
2. Resolve `Tool/P0004-*` or `Extension/E0001-*` under `E:\Dev`.
3. Verify/build → bump **minor** → commit only (no push).

Report: version, SHA, files committed.

# /hub-ui — Hub UI stack (một lệnh, đủ rule/skill/package/script)

**Mục tiêu:** Không cần @ từng file `.mdc` / skill / README — agent đọc **một bundle** rồi chạy script.

## Cú pháp

```
/hub-ui
/hub-ui P0006 overview
/hub-ui P0020 notes
/hub-ui catalog
```

| Lệnh | Ý nghĩa |
|------|---------|
| `/hub-ui` | Refresh package + agent manifest; đọc catalog AGENTS.md |
| `/hub-ui P0006 overview` | Trên + clone screen (vendor, theme, tsc, checklist layer 3) |
| `/hub-ui catalog` | Chỉ liệt kê stack (không sửa code) — trả bảng từ `AGENTS.md` |

## Agent — làm ngay (theo thứ tự)

| 4 | `packages/hub-ui/README.md` |

Chỉ khi cần chi tiết migrate: `.cursor/skills/sync-p0004-ui-shell/reference.md` § Clone 100%.

**Không** gọi riêng `p0004-ui-sync` agent — trùng skill trên.

### 2. Chạy script (terminal)

```powershell
node E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\hub-ui-stack.cjs
# có tool + screen:
node E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\hub-ui-stack.cjs P0006 overview
```

Tương đương: `sync-hub-ui-vendor` + `P0004 a

items.name: /onboard-tool
items.bodyPreview:
# Onboard tool (import external repo → P00xx)

Import a GitHub repo into `E:\Dev\Tool`, assign workspace code, scaffold manifest/docs, sync P0004 catalog.

## When to use

- Pull / clone repo về workspace
- Gán mã P00xx, chuẩn hóa tool mới
- Đồng bộ repo ngoài vào Tool Hub catalog

**Not for:** Git / Push / Release → use `/release` or `Release P00xx`.

**“Làm hết đề xuất” / “toàn bộ đề xuất”:** Phase B (Tailwind, rename, domain, …) + **Release** via `p00xx-ship-keywords`.

## Steps

1. Read `.cursor/skills/p00xx-tool-onboard/SKILL.md` (full checklist).
2. Read `E:\Dev\Tool\TOOL-CODES.md` for next code.
3. **Preflight:** `E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\onboard-preflight.ps1 -Slug {Slug}`
4. Phase A: clone → scaffold → build → validate manifest → `scan:local`.
5. Phase B (if user asked): rename, domain, Tailwind, code-split — see skill.
6. Phase C (if user asked ship): hand off to `p00xx-ship-keywords` + `p00xx-release-workflow`.

## Validate before Hub scan

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -File "E:\Dev\.cursor\skills\p00xx-tool-onboard\scripts\validate-manifest.ps1" `
  -ManifestPath "E:\Dev\Tool\{CODE}-{Slug}\tool.manifest.json"
```

## Hub commit scope

Only stage scan outputs in P0004-

items.name: /push
items.bodyPreview:
# Push — P00xx / E00xx

Keyword: **`Push P0004`** (= Git + push).

## Run

1. Read `.cursor/skills/p00xx-ship-keywords/SKILL.md` — keyword **Push**.
2. Verify/build → commit (**minor**) → push GitHub (`gh auth status`, Cursor browser OAuth if needed).

No deploy, no GitHub Release, no **major** bump unless user also said Release.

Report: version, branch, remote, push result.

items.name: /release
items.bodyPreview:
# Release (P00xx)

Keyword: **`Release P0004`** (= full autonomous pipeline — no step-by-step prompts).

## Run (autonomous)

```powershell
powershell -File E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\ship-product.ps1 -Code P0004 -Keyword Release
```

Order: auto-commit WIP (default) → `check-version-sync` → build → push → Vercel READY + HTTP smoke → `gh release` → `post-gh-release` → `scan:local`.

## Agent checklist

1. Read `.cursor/skills/p00xx-ship-keywords/SKILL.md` — keyword **Release**.
2. Ensure version triple + CHANGELOG top entry (real Changes) before script.
3. **P0005:** `zalo-ai-bot-release` for VPS deploy/smoke after push.
4. Report full ship block from `p00xx-ship-keywords` when done.

items.name: /sync-hub-ui
items.bodyPreview:
# /sync-hub-ui — Clone một screen (alias của `/hub-ui`)

**Khuyến nghị lần đầu / audit full stack:** dùng **`/hub-ui`** — đọc `AGENTS.md` + rule + skill + package README một lượt.

## Usage

```
/sync-hub-ui P0006 overview
/sync-hub-ui P0006 all
```

## Script

```powershell
node E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\sync-hub-ui-screen.cjs P0006 overview
```

Hoặc kèm refresh package + manifest:

```powershell
node E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\hub-ui-stack.cjs P0006 overview
```

Chi tiết layer 3 + exports: `.cursor/commands/hub-ui.md` và `packages/hub-ui/README.md`.

items.name: /verify-browser
items.bodyPreview:
# Verify in browser (fix until done)

Run the **fix → browser verify → repeat** loop until the task passes or is blocked.

## Steps

1. Read `.cursor/skills/browser-verify-until-done/SKILL.md`.
2. Read `.cursor/rules/dev-workspace.mdc` §3.
3. Identify URL(s) and acceptance criteria for this task.
4. Use **Cursor browser MCP** (Chrome 0010 only if MCP cannot cover the flow).
5. Loop: edit code → verify in browser → fix again if failing (max ~5 rounds, then report blocker).
6. End with: Verified URL, steps, pass/fail, and `Version: X.Y.Z → A.B.C` if product code changed.

Do not mark the task complete after code-only changes without a successful browser check.

| Cursor command | Script | Đọc gì (agent) | Chạy gì |

```powershell

cd E:\Dev\Tool\P0004-Tool-Hub
pnpm hub-ui:stack
pnpm hub-ui:stack -- P0006 overview   # nếu thêm wrapper; hoặc:

```

---

## Rules

items.name: Hub keyboard shortcuts
items.summary: F Search · N New · E Edit selection · Esc close modal
items.bodyPreview:
# Hub keyboard shortcuts (workspace standard)

Synced across P0004 Hub UI via `@tool-workspace/hub-ui`.

| Key | Action | Where |
|-----|--------|--------|
| **F** | Focus main tab search (FilterBar) | All Hub list tabs with FilterBar |
| **N** | Add / create (primary row action) | Users → Add; screens with `onNew` handler |
| **E** | Edit selection (when rows checked) | Users → Edit; Hub → Refresh selected tools |
| **Esc** | Close open modal | Tool detail, user access, agent context, Supabase detail |

## Implementation

- Module: `packages/hub-ui/src/keyboard/hub-keyboard-shortcuts.ts`
- App shell: `setHubActiveScreen("library" | "users" | "system")` on sidebar change (`App.tsx`)
- Search: `FilterBar` with `shortcutScope` matching active tab
- N / E: `useHubPageShortcuts("library", { … })` per screen (keep-mounted safe)
- Esc: each modal `useEffect` on `Escape` (unchanged)

## Tab Hub (`library`)

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| F | Focus Hub search |
| E | Refresh **selected tools** (tick rows first; needs `onRefreshTool`) |
| N | — (not wired on Hub catalog) |

## Not global

- Does not fire inside `<input>`, `<textarea>`, or `contenteditable`
- **N** / **E** only when the

items.summary: "Full reference: version tiers, ship pipeline, CHANGELOG, env, Hub UI (read when needed)."
items.bodyPreview:
# Dev workspace — full reference (agent)

**Compact gate (Always Apply):** `.cursor/rules/dev-workspace-compact.mdc`  
**Source of truth (detail):** this file  
**Human review (Vietnamese):** `E:\Dev\.cursor\RULES-HUMAN-VI.md` — keep in sync when policy changes.  
**Release pipeline:** `E:\Dev\Rules\RELEASE-STANDARD.md` · `Tool/scripts/ship-product.ps1`

---

## 0. VERSION BUMP — hard gate (read first, every task)

When you change **meaningful code or UI** in `Tool/P00xx-*` or `Extension/E00xx-*`:

1. **Before** final reply / commit / push: bump the correct tier; sync `package.json`, `tool.manifest.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, extension manifests as applicable.
2. **Last line of summary** (required): `Version: X.Y.Z → A.B.C` — or `Version: no bump (read-only / whitespace only)`.
3. **Never** wait for the user to say “nhớ bump version”.
4. A **`stop` hook** may follow up if git shows code changes without version files — treat as blocking.
5. Product extras: `P0021-.../p0021-version-bump.mdc`, `E0001-.../e0001-auto-bump-version.mdc`.

| Tier | When | Bump |
|------|------|------|
| **Patch** | Task done with meaningful code/UI change | `PATCH + 1` |
| **Minor** | User requests **commit** (

items.name: dev-workspace-compact
items.summary: "Always-on: Vietnamese + 3 proposals + Version line + browser verify (compact gate)."
items.bodyPreview:
# Dev workspace — compact gate (always on)

Full policy: `.cursor/rules/dev-workspace.mdc` · Human (VI): `.cursor/RULES-HUMAN-VI.md`

## Every reply (§1) — no exceptions

- Reply to the user in **Vietnamese**; Tool/Extension **UI strings** in **English**.
- **Always** end with **3 numbered proposals** (`1.` `2.` `3.`) — even for “done”, “removed X”, or one-line answers. Short task ≠ skip footer.
- Execute proposals only when user says **ok 1**, **ok 1 2**, **ok 1 2 3**, **làm 1**, **tự làm**, etc.
- **Last line** of summary: `Version: X.Y.Z → A.B.C` or `Version: no bump (reason)`.
- **Commit / push / deploy / release** only when user explicitly asks.
- **Hooks:** `afterAgentResponse` audits format; `stop` → `verify-working-rules-on-stop.ps1` may auto follow-up (max 2). Check Cursor → Settings → Hooks → Execution Log if agent “forgets”.

## Version (§0 — when editing product code)

Meaningful changes in `Tool/P00xx-*` or `Extension/E00xx-*` → bump + CHANGELOG block before task end.  
**Stop hook** may auto-follow-up if version files missing. Ship: skill `p00xx-ship-keywords`.

## Browser verify (§3 — UI / bug / deploy)

For UI changes, bugs, extension+web flows, deploy smoke: read s

browser-verify-until-done
items.bodyPreview:
# Browser verify until done

## When this applies

- Changed **UI**, routes, client logic, or styles in `Tool/P00xx-*` / `Extension/E00xx-*`
- User reports a **bug** visible in the app or extension popup
- **Deploy / release** smoke test (with production or local URL)
- User says: *fix xong*, *test browser*, *loop đến khi được*, *smoke test*

**Skip** only when: pure docs, read-only review, server-only change with no UI surface, or user says *skip browser*.

## Browsers (priority)

1. **Cursor IDE browser MCP** — default (`browser_navigate`, `browser_snapshot`, console/network tools)
2. **Chrome profile 0010** — if user already logged in there and MCP cannot reach local host / extension-only flow

Do not ask the user to log in again if a session is already authenticated.

## Fix loop (mandatory)

Repeat until **done** or **blocked**:

```
EDIT → RUN (dev/build if needed) → BROWSER VERIFY → pass?
         ↑                                    |
         └──────── no: diagnose, fix ─────────┘
```

### 1. Prepare

- Know the **URL** (local port from `tool.manifest.json` / README, or production URL).
- Know **acceptance**: what screen, button, or data proves the fix.

### 2. Browser ver

items.name: p00xx-release-workflow
items.trigger: Runs release/deploy for Tool/P00xx. Triggers on Release/Push/Git + product code (e.g. Release P0004), deploy, push, publish, gh release. See p00xx-ship-keywords for Git vs Push vs Release.
items.summary: Runs release/deploy for Tool/P00xx. Triggers on Release/Push/Git + product code (e.g. Release P0004), deploy, push, publish, gh release. See p00xx-ship-keywords for Git vs Push vs Release.
items.bodyPreview:
# P00xx Release Workflow

## Scope

Use this for `Tool/P00xx-*` repositories. It standardizes release work while respecting each repo's own scripts and deployment target.

## Autonomous Release command

When the user says **`Release P00xx`** (or approves release proposals with ok 1 2 3), run the **full pipeline without per-step confirmation**:

```powershell
powershell -File E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\ship-product.ps1 -Code P00xx -Keyword Release
```

Script handles: auto-commit WIP (default) → version check → build → push → production smoke → GitHub release → metadata. Agent still verifies prerequisites (triple sync, CHANGELOG, no secrets) and reports the final ship block.

## Safety Rules

- Only commit, push, deploy, or create releases when the user explicitly asks.
- Never commit secrets or local runtime data: `.env*`, credentials, Supabase service role keys, DB passwords, cookies, VPS env files, backups, private exports, or token-bearing config.
- Preserve unrelated user changes. If the working tree has unrelated edits, leave them out of the commit or ask when separation is impossible.
- Do not use destructive git commands, force-push, skip hooks, or change git config unless the user

items.name: p00xx-ship-keywords
items.bodyPreview:
# P00xx ship keywords (Git · Push · Release)

## Parse the command

Pattern: **`{Git|Push|Release} {CODE}`**

| User says | CODE examples | Resolve path |
|-----------|---------------|--------------|
| `Git P0004` | P0004, P0020, E0001 | `Tool/P0004-*` or `Extension/E0001-*` under `E:\Dev` |
| `Push P0020` | case-insensitive | Match folder by code prefix; read `tool.manifest.json` |

If multiple folders match, pick the canonical name from manifest `code` or ask once.

**P0005** release/deploy: use `zalo-ai-bot-release` skill instead of generic deploy step 6.

---

## Keyword → pipeline (cumulative)

Each level **includes all steps above it**:

| Keyword | Steps | Version tier (§0) |
|---------|--------|-------------------|
| **Git** | 1 verify/build → 2 commit | **Minor** before commit (`MINOR+1`, `PATCH=1`) |
| **Push** | Git + 3 push GitHub | Minor on commit; **no Major** unless user also said Release |
| **Release** | Push + 4 deploy → 5 smoke → 6 tag + `gh release` | **Major** before official push to `main` (`MAJOR+1`, `MINOR=1`, `PATCH=1`) |

**Patch** is separate: bump when **finishing a code task**, before any Git/Push/Release keyword.

### Release = run full pipeline autono

items.name: p00xx-tool-onboard
items.bodyPreview:
# P00xx tool onboard (import + chuẩn hóa)

Bring an external repo into `E:\Dev\Tool` with workspace conventions, then hand off to ship skills for Git/Push/Release.

**Not in scope:** ongoing release/deploy → `p00xx-ship-keywords` + `p00xx-release-workflow`.

**User says “làm hết đề xuất” / “toàn bộ đề xuất” after onboard:** run Phase B (if listed) + Phase C **Release** in one flow.

---

## Parse intent

| User says | Phase |
|-----------|-------|
| Pull / clone / sync repo về workspace | A (import) |
| Gán P00xx, chuẩn hóa, onboard | A + catalog |
| Rename folder/repo, domain, Tailwind, code-split | B (hardening) |
| Git / Push / Release P00xx | **Stop** → ship skills |

Inputs to confirm (infer when obvious):

- GitHub URL or `owner/repo`
- Display name and slug (`id` kebab-case)
- Stack type: Web / Desktop / Bot / …
- Deploy target: vercel / vps / local-only

---

## Phase A — Import

```
Onboard progress:
- [ ] A1 Assign P00xx code + folder name
- [ ] A2 Preflight + clone
- [ ] A3 Scaffold docs + manifest + workspace port registry
- [ ] A4 Fix build (pnpm, vite-env, AI Studio cleanup)
- [ ] A5 Validate manifest (no BOM)
- [ ] A6 Hub catalog (scan:local)
- [ ] A7 First commit (u

items.name: sync-p0004-ui-shell
items.trigger: Syncs the P0004 Tool Hub UI shell, design tokens, typography, spacing, effects, sidebar/header/tab patterns, Hub-like data loading (stale-while-revalidate, eager tabs, keep-mounted, build snapshots, background sync), HubLoadingView, FilterBar with faceted counts, badge-registry icons, Catalog/Live source badges, cards, tables, settings, logs, and compact 90% behavior into another P00xx tool while preserving that tool's data, APIs, auth, storage, and business logic. Use when the user asks to copy, đồng bộ, migrate, reuse, or apply P0004 UI/design/shell/loading/filter/Hub tab style to another tool.
items.summary: Syncs the P0004 Tool Hub UI shell, design tokens, typography, spacing, effects, sidebar/header/tab patterns, Hub-like data loading (stale-while-revalidate, eager tabs, keep-mounted, build snapshots, background sync), HubLoadingView, FilterB
items.bodyPreview:
# Sync P0004 UI Shell

## Goal

Apply `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub` as the visual and structural standard for a target tool **without** changing domain behavior, data, or backends.

## When to use

- New P00xx fork or “làm giống Hub / P0004”
- Đồng bộ filter, tab header, font, sidebar, table style, **loading UI**
- Audit tool đích so với Hub (Users, System, Hub list)

## Architecture (what to copy vs share)

| Approach | When |
|----------|------|
| **`packages/hub-ui` + theme sync** (recommended) | P0006-style workspace; run `sync-hub-ui-vendor.cjs` + `sync-hub-theme-from-p0004.cjs` |
| **`vendor/hub-ui` per repo** | P0004, P0020 — same package name, vendored copy |

Start with **copy + adapt** using the manifest in [reference.md](reference.md).

## Workflow

0. **Refresh shared package** (if P0004 shell changed): `node E:\Dev\Tool\scripts\sync-hub-ui-vendor.cjs` (+ `--target` for other tools).
1. **Identify** source `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub`, target `Tool/P00xx-*`.
2. **Read** [reference.md](reference.md) manifest; open listed source files.
3. **Read target**: `package.json`, entry, theme, layout, existing filters/tabs.
4. **Plan** (short): shell → tokens → tabs → **Hub-like load** → filters

items.name: sync-p0004-ui-shell/reference
items.summary: P0004 UI clone checklist and path manifest
items.bodyPreview:
# P0004 UI contract — file manifest

Canonical source: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub`. Copy or adapt presentation only; never replace target business logic.

## Layer map

| Layer | Purpose | P0004 paths |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| Tokens & scale | Colors, compact 90%, control heights | `src/theme/p0008-globals.css`, `src/styles.css` |
| Icon scale | Lucide sizes at 0.9× | `src/lib/ui-scale.ts` (`compactIconSize`) |
| App shell | Sidebar, top chrome, screen routing | `src/App.tsx`, `src/components/sales-shell/Sidebar.tsx` |
| Tab header | Sticky title, refresh, settings, display prefs | `src/components/sales-shell/AppTabHeader.tsx` |
| Loading UI | Orb via portal `#hub-tab-loader-root`; one viewport center | `HubLoaderRoot.tsx`, `HubLoadingView.tsx`, `SystemTabLoadingView.tsx` |
| Tab perf | Eager routes, keep-mounted, boot prefetch | `App.tsx` (`visitedScreens`), `hub-background-prefetch.ts`, `SystemHubScreen.tsx` |
| Data loading | SWR cache, snapshots, priority API | See § Data loading contract below |
| Source badges | Catalog vs Live (phased metrics) | `SupabaseMetricsSourceBadge.tsx`, `supabase-project-metrics-source.ts` |
| System sub-tabs | Overview / Schema / Quota / Temp

items.name: tool-icon-identity-workflow

Use when the user asks to scan icons, design tool icons, create 5 icon samples, chốt icon, đồng bộ icon, or replace default product icons.
items.summary: Finds missing product identity icons, designs 5 review variants, and synchronizes approved SVG icons across P0004 Tool Hub, P00xx tools, E00xx extensions, manifests, favicons, and app/extension branding. Use when the user asks to scan icons
items.bodyPreview:
# Tool Icon Identity Workflow

## Scope

Use this skill for product identity icons for `Tool/P00xx-*` and `Extension/E00xx-*`.

Do not use it for generic UI icons, table glyphs, third-party brand icons, or route/status icons unless the user explicitly asks.

## Source Of Truth

- Tool Hub icon assets: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/public/icons/tools/<CODE>.svg`
- Tool Hub mapping: `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/src/lib/visual.ts`
- Product manifests: `tool.manifest.json`
- Web app icon targets: app metadata, favicon, public icon assets
- Extension icon targets: `manifest.json` `icons` and `action.default_icon`, popup/header branding

## Required Workflow

1. Scan inventory:
   - Read `Tool/P*/tool.manifest.json` and `Extension/E*/tool.manifest.json`.
   - Extract `code`, `name`, `type`, `status`, and optional `icon`.
   - Compare with `Tool/P0004-Tool-Hub/public/icons/tools/<CODE>.svg`.
   - Check whether the source repo also has its own app/extension icon path.

2. Report gaps before editing:
   - `missing-hub-icon`: no `<CODE>.svg` in P0004.
   - `missing-manifest-icon`: manifest has no `icon`.
   - `missing-source-icon`: source repo has no favicon/app/extension icon.
   - `needs-review`: icon exis

items.name: workspace-design-preview-gate
items.bodyPreview:
# Workspace design template gate

## When to use

- User **explicitly** asks for design review, 5 mockups, Design Template, or `Design: V1`…`V5`
- **Do not** scaffold design previews proactively for every new feature

## Rules

1. **On-demand only** — no design-first gate before production unless the user requests it.
2. **In-tab only** — mockups live under **System → Design Template** (`/system/template`), with the existing 5-card picker + inline preview (not separate `/design-preview/*` routes unless the user asks).
3. **≥5 distinct variants** per feature; static mock data; clone existing Hub shells when relevant (e.g. `modal-shell--tool-detail` + TOC).
4. **After lock** — when the user says `Design: V1`…`V5` (or uses Lock in the tab):
   - Delete `*-mock.tsx`, preview-only CSS, and registry entries for that feature
   - Update `design-registry.ts` / remove feature from active list
   - Tab shows **No active designs** until the next review is requested
   - Keep production implementation only; no durable “approved design archive” in app code unless asked
5. Document `docs/DESIGN-PREVIEW-{feature}.md` during review; optional `docs/DESIGN-DECISION.md` after lock.

## P0004 Tool Hub
